channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California officials warn against price gouging as rents soar in fire-stricken Los Angeles
item.description: Southern California's expensive housing market is going to get a lot more competitive after deadly firestorms torched more than 12,000 homes and other structures in the Los Angeles area, leaving tens of thousands of people without a place to stay.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Swift returns to Arrowhead to watch Travis Kelce and the Chiefs, joined by Caitlin Clark
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Pop superstar Taylor Swift returned to Arrowhead Stadium to cheer on her boyfriend, Travis Kelce, and the rest of the Kansas City Chiefs on Saturday as they face the Houston Texans in the divisional round of the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NYC mayor defends private meeting with incoming President Trump
item.description: New York City’s Democratic mayor is defending his private meeting with President-elect Donald Trump as the Republican prepares to retake the White House.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area.
item.dc:creator: Jack Dura And Julie Walker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Need to get a tattoo removed? What to know about the risks, the cost and more
item.description: When you’re young and carefree, a spur-of-the-moment tattoo feels like the ultimate form of self-expression. Years later, though, you might have a change of heart.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California is years behind in implementing a law to make homes more fire resistant
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Reeling from destructive wildfires, including the deadliest in California history, state lawmakers in 2020 passed new requirements for clearing combustible materials like dead plants and wooden furniture within 5 feet (1.5 meters) of homes in risky areas.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recalled Oakland mayor indicted on federal corruption charges
item.description: Sheng Thao, the former recalled mayor of Oakland, California, faces corruption charges related to her boyfriend and two others, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Will spring have showers? NOAA releases weather predictions for California
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Joan Plowright, Tony Award-winning British actor and widow of Laurence Olivier, dies at 95
item.description: Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, who with her late husband Laurence Olivier did much to revitalize the U.K.'s theatrical scene in the decades after World War II, has died. She was 95.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, Fox 5

item.title: Bail denied for American captured in France and accused of assaulting Pennsylvania student
item.description: EAST BERLIN, Pa. (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was ordered held without bail Friday during a court hearing in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted expected to fill JD Vance's Senate seat
item.description: DeWine is expected to announce Friday that he is selecting Jon Husted to fill the Senate seat vacated by JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Feral swine to be removed from Land Between the Lakes
item.description: Feral swine, a non-native species, are not only threatening visitor safety and cultural sites, but are also digging up native plants.
item.dc:creator: Davis Nolan, NewsNation

item.title: Q&A: 'God Bless the USA' singer Lee Greenwood on Donald Trump's inauguration and Bibles
item.description: Forty years ago, Lee Greenwood released “God Bless the U.S.A.,” a patriotic anthem that has become a standard at political gatherings. He's performed the song for a number of presidents, including Ronald Reagan and George H. W. Bush.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok says it will 'go dark' unless it gets clarity from Biden following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: TikTok says it will have to “go dark” this weekend unless the outgoing Biden administration assures the company it won’t enforce a shutdown of the popular app after the Supreme Court unanimously upheld the federal law banning the app unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Fire at one of the world's largest battery plants forces evacuations in California
item.description: Hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate and part of Highway 1 in Northern California was closed after a major fire erupted at one of the world's largest battery storage plants.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: Biden administration picks 15 more drugs for Medicare negotiation
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) has selected 15 more drugs for Medicare price negotiation, announcing the selection two weeks ahead of schedule.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Girls basketball coach accused of hitting person with car after argument
item.description: A girls' youth basketball coach is accused of hitting a person with his vehicle after an argument following a game Saturday in Columbia, according to state police, and it was caught on surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US recovers $31 million in Social Security payments to dead people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government clawed back more than $31 million in Social Security payments that improperly went to dead people, a recovery that one official said Wednesday was “just the tip of the iceberg.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
